prompt: A candid, badly timed snapshot aesthetic taken from too close and slightly behind, as if someone accidentally photographed a person at the worst possible moment in a dark nightclub. The image is vertically framed and heavily motion-blurred, with the subject filling almost the entire frame. Show only 1 person, turned away from the camera so the face is not visible, with long wavy rainbow pastel hair transitioning from hot pink and orange at the crown into yellow, green, cyan, blue, and violet through the lengths. The person wears a pale blush-pink top or hoodie, softly lit and also blurred by movement. The composition should feel awkward and intrusive, with the camera too close, the hair dominating the left and center of the frame, and only a partial shoulder and back visible near the bottom right. In the dark background, include 1 dim nightclub bar area with scattered colored lights, shiny bottles, and vague reflective highlights on the right side, all out of focus. Use harsh mixed club lighting, low-light digital camera noise, flash-like exposure on the hair and clothing, strong blur, shallow detail, and an unflattering accidental-phone-photo vibe. Make it feel like a forbidden saved snapshot, messy, spontaneous, and realistically imperfect.
